Scenario: Verify grid refreshes automatically after a set interval
Steps:
1. Open the CF Grid page
2. Wait for the configured interval
ExpectedResult: Grid data reloads without user action

Scenario: Verify auto-refresh interval is configurable
Steps:
1. Check settings for refresh interval
2. Change interval value
3. Reload page
ExpectedResult: Grid refreshes at new interval

Scenario: Verify auto-refresh can be disabled
Steps:
1. Locate auto-refresh toggle
2. Disable it
ExpectedResult: Grid stops refreshing automatically — only refreshes on manual action

Scenario: Verify auto-refresh resumes after re-enabling
Steps: 1. Disable then re-enable auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Grid resumes auto-refreshing at configured interval

Scenario: Verify auto-refresh does not interrupt user interaction
Steps:
1. Start editing a row
2. Wait for refresh interval to pass
ExpectedResult: Refresh does not reset or cancel active edits

Scenario: Verify newly added records appear after refresh
Steps:
1. Add a record in another session or tab
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: New record appears in grid without manual reload

Scenario: Verify updated records show latest values after refresh
Steps:
1. Modify a record externally
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Grid cell shows updated value

Scenario: Verify deleted records are removed from grid after refresh
Steps:
1. Delete a record externally
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Record no longer visible in grid

Scenario: Verify grid row count updates after refresh
Steps:
1. Add/remove records
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Row count in grid and pagination matches new total

Scenario: Verify sort order is maintained after refresh
Steps:
1. Sort grid by a column
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Sort order preserved after data refreshes

Scenario: Verify active filters are preserved after refresh
Steps:
1. Apply a column filter
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Filter still applied — only matching rows shown

Scenario: Verify current page stays selected after refresh
Steps:
1. Navigate to page 3 of grid
2. Wait for auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Grid stays on page 3 after refresh

Scenario: Verify a visual indicator shows when refresh is in progress
Steps: 1. Watch grid during auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Spinner or subtle loading indicator visible during refresh

Scenario: Verify grid does not flicker or blank out during refresh
Steps: 1. Watch grid during auto-refresh
ExpectedResult: Data remains visible — smooth update without blank flash

Scenario: Verify last refreshed timestamp is shown on grid
Steps: 1. Open CF Grid page
ExpectedResult: Timestamp shows when data was last refreshed

Scenario: Verify manual refresh button triggers immediate refresh
Steps: 1. Click manual refresh button
ExpectedResult: Grid data reloads immediately

Scenario: Verify grid handles server error during refresh gracefully
Steps: 1. Simulate server error during refresh
ExpectedResult: Error message shown — previous data preserved

Scenario: Verify grid retries after a failed refresh attempt
Steps:
1. Cause refresh to fail once
2. Wait for next interval
ExpectedResult: Grid retries and loads data on next cycle

Scenario: Verify user is notified if refresh fails repeatedly
Steps: 1. Cause multiple consecutive refresh failures
ExpectedResult: Warning or notification shown to user

Scenario: Verify auto-refresh does not cause memory leak over extended use
Steps:
1. Leave CF Grid open for 30+ minutes
2. Monitor browser memory
ExpectedResult: Memory usage stable — no continuous increase

Scenario: Verify refresh response time is within acceptable limit (under 3 sec)
Steps: 1. Monitor network during refresh
ExpectedResult: Refresh completes within 3 seconds under normal load
